text: Altool optimization « Create instruction files: Write detailed instructions for your Al in appropriate fles (cursorrules, windsurrules, claude.md) « Local documentation: Download API documentation to your project foder for accuracy « Use multiple tools: Some founders run both Cursor and Windsurf simultaneously on the same project « Tool specialization: Cursor s a bit faster for frontend work, while Windsurf think slonger « Compare outputs: Generate muliple solutions and pick the best one Complex feature development « Create standalone prototypes: Build complex features i clean codebase first « Use reference implementations: Point the Al to working examples (o follow. o Clear boundarles: Maintain consistent external APIs while allowing internal changes. « Modular architecture: Service-based architectures with clear boundaries work beter than monorepos Tech stack considerations o Established frameworks excel: Ruby on Ralls works well due to 20 years of consistent conventions * Training data matters: Newer languages like Rust or Elixr may have less training data « Modularity s key: Small, modular files are easier for both humans and Als to work with o Avold large files: Don' have fles that are thousands of nes long Beyond coding « DevOps automation: Use Al for configuring servers, DNS, and hosting « Design assistance: Generate favicons and other design elements o Content creation: Draft documentation and marketing materials « Educational tool: Ask the Al to explain implementations line by line. « Use screenshots: Share Ul bugs or design inspiration visually « Voice input: Tools like Aqua enable 140 words per minute input Continuous improvement « Regular refactoring: Once tests are In place,refactor frequently « Identify opportunities: Ask the Al to find refactoring candidates o Stay current: Try every new model release « Recognize strengths: Different models excel a different tasks
